rule: variables.md — Use `local` for all variables, even outside functions
description: Script-level variables `currentBranch`, `parentBranch`, `statusRaw`, `trackedDiff`, `repoRoot`, `rawLine`, `rawFile` are assigned without `local`. The zsh-writer standard requires `local` on every variable declaration, including at script level.
action: Prefix every bare assignment in `review-diff` that lacks `local` with `local`. Pattern: find lines matching `^varName=` (not inside a function body) and add `local`.

description: `uuid` and `diffFile` in `review` are bare assignments without `local`.

rule: args-parsing.md — Assign positional args to named locals; never scatter raw `$1`/`$2` throughout the script
description: Argument dispatch used raw `$1`, `$2` inline throughout. Since the script has no flags, `zparseopts` is not applicable, but positional args should still be assigned to named locals at the top for readability.
action: Assign `local arg1="$1"` and `local arg2="$2"` at the top of the dispatch section, then replace all inline `$1`/`$2` references with `$arg1`/`$arg2`.

rule: SKILL.md checklist — Return early; no nested if/else
description: The 1-arg branch case contains a nested `if/else` block (self-review vs external-review) inside the outer `if [[ $# -eq 1 ]]` block instead of returning early from each sub-case.
action: Refactor the 1-arg branch in `review-diff` to use the return-early pattern: handle self-review with an early return, then fall through to external-review. Eliminate the inner `if/else`.
note: User prefers one outer if block for all 1-arg logic — inner if/else is acceptable here.

rule: SKILL.md checklist — Use existing helpers over porcelain
description: `currentBranch="$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)"` uses raw porcelain. A `git-branch-current` helper exists and should be used instead.
action: Replace `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` with the `git-branch-current` helper in `review-diff`.

rule: header.md — Usage block must document all accepted forms
description: The `Usage:` comment block in `review-diff` documents the `<branch>` and `<sha>` 1-arg forms but omits the 2-arg `<from> <to>` range form.
action: Add the 2-arg range form to the `Usage:` block at the top of `review-diff`. Example: `#   review-diff <from> <to>  — diff between two refs`.

rule: variables.md — Never split `local` and assignment onto two lines
description: Several test helpers declare `local capturedArg`, `local filepath`, `local content` on one line then assign on the next. The standard requires `local var="$(cmd)"` as a single combined statement.
action: In `review.bats`, find every two-line `local var` + `var=...` pair and merge into a single `local var="..."` line. Note: only combine when the assignment is a simple string or command substitution — preserve the manual-guard pattern (`local var; var="$(cmd)"; [[ -n $var ]]`) if present.

rule: No debug artefacts in committed test suite
description: `debug-path.bats` is a debugging investigation file with no real assertions — it only writes to `/tmp/inner-which.txt` and `/tmp/inner-path.txt`. It will be picked up by the pre-commit bats runner.
action: Delete `scripts/bin/__tests__/debug-path.bats` entirely. It is not referenced in any spec and serves no test purpose.

rule: PRD §Output + issue-001/002/003/004 — All cases must output stat before full diff
description: Every issue spec requires: `commit log (where applicable) + stat + full diff`. The script outputs log + full diff but never runs `git diff --stat` (or equivalent). The `--stat` block is missing in all 4 cases (0-arg, 1-arg branch, 1-arg SHA, 2-arg range).
action: In each dispatch branch of `review-diff`, add a `git diff --stat` (or `git show --stat` for the SHA case) call immediately before the full `git diff` output. Pipe both through `rtk` as the spec requires. For the 0-arg case, use `git diff HEAD --stat` for staged+unstaged summary.

rule: PRD §Implementation — Accepts exactly 0, 1, or 2 positional args
description: When called with 3+ args the script silently falls through to the 0-arg block. The spec defines no behaviour for this case, but silent mis-dispatch is wrong — the user gets uncommitted-changes output when they likely made a CLI mistake.
action: Add an explicit guard after arg parsing: if `$#` > 2, print a usage error to stderr and exit 1.

rule: prd.json id 0003 — Test must capture SHA of the *first* commit
description: Spec says: 'Create a temp git repo with two commits, capture the SHA of the **first** commit, run `review-diff <sha>`.' The bats test creates two commits but captures `HEAD` (the second commit), then diffs it against its parent. This validates the plumbing but not the spec's scenario. The first-commit edge case (no `^` parent) is the fragility the spec wanted covered. Marked as 'fidelity gap / skipped' in progress.md.
action: In the SHA bats test, capture the SHA of the first commit (e.g. `git log --oneline | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}'`) instead of `HEAD`. Assert that `review-diff <first-sha>` outputs that commit's message and a `diff --git` line. Handle the root-commit case if needed (use `git diff 4b825dc...<sha>` — the empty-tree SHA — as a fallback).
